resources.string: Convert .hbc bytecode into readable .hasm assembly files
Made by FW · HBC to Bytecode
Select an index.android.bundle (or any .hbc) and an output folder. Tap Run in App or copy the command for Termux.
Select the .hasm folder and output file. Tap Run in App or copy the command for Termux.
Select .hasm files, choose keywords and search.
The app uses Termux Python to run hbctool.\nInstall it automatically with one tap.

Python not found.

In Termux run:
 pkg install python

Then come back here.
Python found! Tap Install to set up hbctool.

Requires Termux with Python:

 pkg install python
 pip install hbctool

The app can install hbctool automatically via Setup.
Something went wrong. Copy the error and report it.

This app lets you disassemble and assemble Hermes Bytecode files (.hbc) used by React Native apps on Android.
The main file you\'ll work with is usually called:
index.android.bundle
This guide walks you through all features step by step.
⚠ Use Termux from F-Droid, not the Play Store. The Play Store version is outdated and may not work correctly.

The app uses Termux to run Python and hbctool.
1. Install Termux from F-Droid (not Play Store) 2. Open Termux and run:
pkg install python
3. Come back here and tap Setup on the main screen.
The app will install hbctool automatically.

After installing Python in Termux:
1. Tap Setup on the main screen 2. The app auto-detects Termux 3. If not found, enter the path manually: /data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin 4. Tap Install hbctool 5. Wait for completion 6. Tap Continue
You only need to do this once.
💡 If the app cannot find Termux automatically, paste the path manually and tap Apply.

To disassemble a Hermes bundle:
1. Tap Disassemble on the main screen 2. Tap Select .hbc File 3. Select your index.android.bundle 4. Tap Select Output Folder 5. Tap Run in App
The log shows progress in real time.
💡 Use an empty folder as output to avoid mixing files from different bundles.

After disassembly you\'ll find .hasm files with Hermes instructions like:
LoadParam r0, 0 GetById r1, r0, 1, \"token\" Call r2, r1, 1
Edit these with any text editor to patch the app\'s behavior. Use Hermes Finder to search for specific strings or opcodes.

After editing your .hasm files:
1. Tap Assemble on the main screen 2. Select the .hasm folder 3. Name the output index.android.bundle 4. Tap Run in App
Then replace the original bundle in the APK and re-sign.
⚠ All .hasm files in the folder will be assembled. Do not mix files from different bundles.

Hermes Finder searches inside .hasm files:
1. Tap Hermes Finder 2. Select one or more .hasm files 3. Pick predefined keywords OR type your own 4. Tap Search
Results show file name, line number and the matching line with keyword highlighted.
💡 You can select multiple .hasm files at once. Results are grouped by file for easy navigation.

Useful things to search for:
• token, apiKey, secret - find API credentials • LoadConstString - all string literals • JmpTrue / JmpFalse - conditional branches (license checks) • AsyncFunction - async calls • fetch - network calls • require - module imports

• Open in Termux generates the hbctool command for manual use • If hbctool reports unsupported version, you need a newer hbctool • Worm border wiggles (~) when processing • Theme follows device setting automatically • Tap Setup → Rescan if Termux is not detected
💡 Always back up your original .hbc file before modifying. Assembly cannot be undone.

HBC to Bytecode is a tool for disassembling and assembling Hermes Bytecode (.hbc) files used by React Native Android apps. Built for Android developers and reverse engineers who work without a PC, directly from their device.
